text: If I ___ more time, I would travel the world.
explain: Second conditional: 'If' + past simple → 'would' + base form.

text: She has been working here ___ 2015.
explain: 'Since' is used with a point in time; 'for' with durations.

text: By the time we arrived, the movie ___ started.
explain: Past perfect describes an action completed before another past action.

text: He is the best player ___ our team.
explain: We say 'the best in a group/team/class'.

text: I'm not used to ___ up so early.
explain: 'Used to + -ing' means being accustomed to something.

text: She speaks English ___ than her brother.
explain: Comparative form of the adverb 'well' is 'better'.

text: I wish I ___ how to play the guitar.
explain: 'Wish' about present situations uses the past simple.

text: The report ___ by the manager tomorrow.
explain: Passive voice in the future: will + be + past participle.

text: Neither my sister ___ I enjoy horror films.
explain: Use 'neither...nor' as a correlative pair.

text: The children were ___ tired to finish their homework.
explain: 'Too + adjective + to + verb' expresses excess preventing an action.

text: I look forward ___ you soon.
explain: 'Look forward to' is followed by a gerund (-ing form).

text: This is the man ___ car was stolen.
explain: 'Whose' shows possession with people.

text: We ___ lived in this city for three years.
explain: Present perfect with 'we' takes 'have'.

text: If you heat water to 100°C, it ___.
explain: Zero conditional describes general truths with present simple in both clauses.

text: There isn't ___ milk left in the fridge.
explain: 'Much' is used with uncountable nouns in negatives.

text: She asked me where ___.
explain: Indirect questions use normal word order and backshift tense.

text: The museum ___ every day except Mondays.
explain: Simple present for routine schedules.

text: I haven't seen her ___ last week.
explain: 'Since' marks the starting point of a continuing period.

text: He ___ to the gym every morning before work.
explain: Simple present with third person singular adds -s/-es.

text: I'd rather you ___ smoke in the house.
explain: 'I'd rather' + subject + past simple expresses a preference.
